Also, Claude has been tricky to serve on insight, but nothing our engineering overlords can't fix. Really thoughtful questions and we will report back to you soon with both these fixed!

Scribble Network

@narek\_keshishyan We don't have enough data yet tbh. But when the feature rolls out, we can start to see more - will keep you posted.

Narek Keshishyan

@kaavya\_prasad That's the honest answer at this stage, fair enough. When it does roll out, the early signal I'd watch isn't total mentions — it's mentions on the questions a human would've answered "it depends," because that's exactly where an AI recommendation displaces a search instead of echoing one. That's the line where consumer GEO either earns its keep or doesn't.

Dipankar Sarkar [...] Narek Keshishyan

@kaavya\_prasad  That ARPU line is the honest cut, and it matches what I see from the consumer-mobile side, AI engines basically never send a referrer, so the whole loop is invisible to attribution. Where I'd push gently: for a consumer app the value of AI visibility shows up as being the thing the model names when someone asks it for a recommendation in a category, not as a referral click. That's real demand, it just never lands in GA or GSC because it's zero-click and usually happens a conversation before the install. Your branded-search correlation idea is probably the closest proxy anyone gets until attribution catches up, are you seeing branded lift actually track AI mentions in early data, or is it still too noisy to call?

kaavya prasad
Scribble Network

results.title: Scribble - LinkedIn
results.content: important. Read the full guide below.  [Likes: 1, Comments: 1]; Scribble (1w): A strong product is not enough if AI search cannot find it. That was the problem RocketX came in with. RocketX had a clear differentiator: a cross-chain aggregator combining CEX and DEX liquidity in one interface. But across high-intent prompts on ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Perplexity, and Copilot, that positioning was barely showing up. So we ran a 15-day GEO sprint. Through Scribble Network, 162 independent creators published 358 pieces of content across X, Substack, Medium, Reddit, and Paragraph. The goal was simple: Make RocketX’s hybrid CEX + DEX model impossible to miss inside AI search. The results: • 9% AI Share of Voice from near-zero • 123 tracked queries covered • 38 citations and 113 mentions • [...] Scribble Network is a global community of creators, researchers, and storytellers working together to make complex ideas more accessible through high-quality content. Operating at the intersection of advertising and emerging technology, we collaborate with tech, fintech, and internet-native brands to turn products, protocols, and digital trends into clear, engaging narratives. Instead of traditional influencer marketing, Scribble Network runs structured creator campaigns that reward clarity, originality, and depth — not just audience size. For creators, Scribble Network offers transparent earning opportunities, thoughtful briefs, and a peer community focused on craft, learning, and shared growth. For brands, it’s a way to work with informed creators who can explain, contextualize, and [...] with informed creators who can explain, contextualize, and humanize their products with credibility. At its core, Scribble Network aligns incentives between builders and storytellers. Fostering a community where better understanding leads to stronger ecosystems.

results.title: How Scribble Network Gets Creator Data Without APIs or Scraping
results.content:
# How Scribble Network Gets Creator Data Without APIs or Scraping

Feb 6, 2026

How Scribble Network Gets Creator Data Without APIs or Scraping

Social Media VerificationClippingMarketingUGC

## What does Scribble do?

Scribble Network is a creator marketplace. They connect brands with creators across X, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube. Over 50,000 creators. 80+ brand partnerships.

To match the right creator with the right brand, you need real data:

 Audience demographics
 Watch time analytics
 Engagement patterns
 Geographic distribution

Follower counts alone don't cut it.

That's all you need to know about Scribble for this post.

## The data access problem

Getting creator data from social platforms is surprisingly hard. There are really only two paths, and neither works well. [...] ### Path 2: Scraping

The alternative most companies turn to. Pull data directly from platforms without permission.

Scraping requires session handling, cookie storage, and accessing data without user consent. GDPR and similar privacy regulations have made this a liability minefield. Beyond legality, scraped data is unreliable. Platforms actively fight scrapers, and one update can break months of development work overnight.

Neither option works for what Scribble needed.

## How does Reclaim solve this?

Here's the insight: the problem isn't that the data doesn't exist. It's that platforms gatekeep access to it. [...] ## How does it work technically?

Here's what happens when a creator onboards:

1. Creator logs into their social platform through Scribble's onboarding. Credentials go directly to Twitter/Instagram/LinkedIn's official login pages. Reclaim never sees passwords.
2. Once logged in, the system pulls relevant analytics from the creator's dashboard.
3. Creator reviews exactly what will be shared and explicitly consents.
4. Reclaim generates a cryptographic proof and shares it with Scribble.

The technology uses Trusted Execution Environments (secure hardware enclaves) and cryptographic attestation. This guarantees the data actually came from the platform and hasn't been tampered with.

If you want the deep technical details, check out our architecture docs.

## Why this is legally sound

May be a Twitter screenshot of phone, screen, timer, thermostat and text that says 'Rain.Trade SelectBrand/Competitor Queries Overview Model History Scribble Cited All sources cited through scribbler's contributions Newest First # #SNo. No. Model SourcesResponse [...] Today, we’re launching the Scribble dashboard.  
  
For the past few months, we’ve been running creator-led AI visibility campaigns and tracking how brands show up across AI search.  
  
One thing became clear very quickly: AI visibility is no longer something brands can manage with screenshots, manual checks, and scattered campaign reports.  
  
Teams need to know:  
  
• What their brand is being cited for?  
  
• Which queries they are missing from?  
  
• Which creators are driving visibility?  
  
• Which AI engines are picking them up?  
  
• How they compare against competitors?  
  
• And what to do next?  
  
That is what the Scribble dashboard is built to answer.
